_doc: Hand-curated entity gold for The Dreaming. Two parts: (1) variantGroups — surface forms that all denote one canonical entity, for the DETERMINISTIC dedup gate (the canonicalize() variant-merge bug; the self-check split 'S5IIX'/'S5 IIX'/'the Panasonic'/'my Lumix S5IIX' into 4 stems). (2) notableEntities — things Josh genuinely engaged with (owned/decided/recurring/opinion), the personal-notability oracle. Grounded by reading ~/Dreaming/articles/*.md and live lucien chunks; NOT derived from bucket-gold (the topic-bin taxonomy this project replaces). See reports/dreaming/gold-curation.md.
_method: Curation by reading: ~/Dreaming/articles (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses, Anamorphic_Lens_Adapters, Claude_Code_and_Skills, Bun_and_TypeScript_Tooling, AI_Memory_and_Agent_Systems, Archie_Project, AlphaPoint_Work_and_Politics, Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment) + sampled lucien messages (the real surface forms Josh typed). No model used.

notableEntities.name: Panasonic Lumix S5IIX
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned full-frame body, bought used for $1,645 in Dec 2024 as his first serious camera; called it 'probably the best value you can ever get in a camera' and uses it daily as an anamorphic + video-conference camera (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: iPhone 15 Pro Max
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned; shoots Apple Log on it and treats it as a legitimate production tool, working out its Quad-Bayer sensor, full-frame-equivalent aperture, and ETTR-inversion behavior in detail (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: L-Mount
notableEntities.whyNotable: The lens mount Josh standardized on; the navigation hub for all his vintage/EF adaptation and recurs across camera conversations (notability.json; Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: M42 mount
notableEntities.whyNotable: Legacy mount he deliberately adapts vintage glass from (Helios 44-2, Jupiter-9, Super-Takumars) onto L-Mount; root of the Frankenstein anamorphic chain (Anamorphic_Lens_Adapters.md; notability.json).

notableEntities.name: EF mount
notableEntities.whyNotable: The other mount he adapts from (adapted EF glass incl. a Canon EF 50mm f/1.2L and telephoto zooms); a recorded decision about which adapters to buy (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md; notability.json).

notableEntities.name: Sigma 150-600
notableEntities.whyNotable: Chosen as the sane long-reach telephoto pick at ~$600 over pricier alternatives; a recorded decision (notability.json).

notableEntities.name: Lumix 75-300
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned telephoto zoom used for reach before the Sigma decision (notability.json).

notableEntities.name: Sankor 16C
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned projection anamorphic adapter used on the S5IIX for de-squeeze experiments; the centerpiece of the Frankenstein chain (L-Mount → M42 → Helios → clamp → Sankor 16C) (Anamorphic_Lens_Adapters.md; lucien chunk).

notableEntities.name: Singer 16-D
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned; he notes 'I have a Singer 16-D, I think its basically the same thing as the Sankor' — same projection-anamorphic optical design (lucien chunk).

notableEntities.name: Helios 44-2
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned M42 taking lens he favors ('i love to take the Helios out'); the cult Helios-plus-anamorphic pairing for swirly bokeh at 58mm (lucien chunk; Anamorphic_Lens_Adapters.md).

notableEntities.name: Jupiter-9
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned M42 portrait lens he adapts for his anamorphic projection lenses ('I mainly use the Jupiter-9 adapted for my anamorphic projection lenses') (lucien chunk).

notableEntities.name: Sirui Aurora
notableEntities.whyNotable: 35mm f/1.4 he was deciding to buy ('I was going to get the Sirui Aurora') to test 35mm against his strong 50mm attachment (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md; lucien chunk).

notableEntities.name: Sirui Saturn 35mm 1.6x
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned modern anamorphic lens ('I already have the Sirui Saturn 35mm 1.6'); part of his catalogued lens library (lucien chunk).

notableEntities.name: Meike 85mm f/1.4
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned AF L-mount portrait lens (seen mounted with the half-cage rig; listed under his AF (L Mount) glass alongside Meike 35mm f/2 and 50mm f/1.8) (lucien chunk).

notableEntities.name: OLPF
notableEntities.whyNotable: Recurring deliberation: the S5IIX ships with no factory OLPF, so he reasoned through adding a Kolari rear-mount magnetic OLPF as 'a class of filtering the camera fundamentally lacks' (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: Apple Log
notableEntities.whyNotable: Shoots Apple Log on the iPhone; worked out its scene-referred log-into-parabola transfer function, P3-ish gamut, and 10-bit ProRes cap as a strict post-grading source (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: V-Log
notableEntities.whyNotable: Shoots V-Log on the S5IIX (~14 stops DR); holds a settled opinion that dual native ISO stays 640/4000 in V-Log and that Live LUT is the only mode treating the camera as film stock (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: DaVinci Resolve
notableEntities.whyNotable: His grading tool; hit and reasoned through the Apple ProRAW/.DNG incompatibility when trying to bring an iPhone raw still into Resolve (Camera_Gear_and_Lenses.md).

notableEntities.name: Bambu A1 Mini
notableEntities.whyNotable: Owned 3D printer that makes the vintage-optics adapter sourcing problem a 'measure → model → print' workflow; the enabler in his vintage-lens/anamorphic dependency chain (Anamorphic_Lens_Adapters.md).

notableEntities.name: PETG
notableEntities.whyNotable: Material he prints with regularly; recurring print-settings discussions and a settled opinion on its tradeoffs vs PLA (notability.json).

notableEntities.name: DIY Variable Diopter Project
notableEntities.whyNotable: His 3D-printed single-focus module building an Iscorama-style rig for ~$50 vs commercial units running into the thousands; solves close-focus collapse on the Sankor chain (Anamorphic_Lens_Adapters.md).

notableEntities.name: Model Context Protocol
notableEntities.whyNotable: Recurring topic: integrates MCP servers across Claude runtimes, debugged the Agent SDK not auto-loading MCP servers in Archie's Telegram path, and self-hosts MCP (Claude_Code_and_Skills.md).

notableEntities.name: Claude Code
notableEntities.whyNotable: Accumulated config, mental models, skills/hooks/slash-command experimentation; the harness layer his Archie work sits above and the subject of a dedicated article (Claude_Code_and_Skills.md).

notableEntities.name: pi
notableEntities.whyNotable: Core runtime pillar he drives both UIs through (web + CLI); part of the OpenClaw → pi → Bun convergence thesis he holds about AI tooling (Bun_and_TypeScript_Tooling.md; project memory).

notableEntities.name: mlx-bun
notableEntities.whyNotable: His own Bun-on-FFI MLX runtime — the project this gold serves; built for bit-exact local inference with no Python intermediary (Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md; Bun_and_TypeScript_Tooling.md).

notableEntities.name: MLX
notableEntities.whyNotable: The framework mlx-bun is built on; recurs constantly and he holds strong opinions about its kernels and lazy eval (notability.json; Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md).

notableEntities.name: mlx-lm
notableEntities.whyNotable: His logit-parity correctness oracle; the Python reference serving stack mlx-bun is validated bit-for-bit against (Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md; CLAUDE.md).

notableEntities.name: OptiQ
notableEntities.whyNotable: The MLX serving framework he settled on for quantized-KV attention and the vision sidecar; mlx-bun's L2 fidelity oracle (Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md).

notableEntities.name: llama.cpp
notableEntities.whyNotable: One of the two Apple-Silicon engines he compared head-to-head, deciding for MLX over llama.cpp for his serving stack — a recorded decision (Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md).

notableEntities.name: Bun
notableEntities.whyNotable: His preferred JS/TS runtime for all new work; strong opinions on its first-party APIs and the Anthropic/Oven acquisition validating his stack-consolidation thesis (Bun_and_TypeScript_Tooling.md).

notableEntities.name: TypeScript
notableEntities.whyNotable: Substantive (not taste) opinion against forced static typing for API-consuming code — the 'rhythm method vs condom' argument; works in it only because projects mandate it (Bun_and_TypeScript_Tooling.md).

notableEntities.name: Postgres
notableEntities.whyNotable: 'The one I'd die on the hill for' — Postgres-only stack thesis (JSONB + GIN + FTS + LISTEN/NOTIFY + pgvector) eating most specialized databases' lunch (Bun_and_TypeScript_Tooling.md).

notableEntities.name: Archie
notableEntities.whyNotable: The internal AI agent platform he built at AlphaPoint (Jira as control plane, producer/verifier separation); his primary AI-lab project, demoed to the board (Archie_Project.md; AlphaPoint_Work_and_Politics.md).

notableEntities.name: Archon
notableEntities.whyNotable: The open-source orchestrator (Cole Medin) Archie is built on; he chose it for its YAML DAG workflows and wrote a custom Jira adapter on top (Archie_Project.md).

notableEntities.name: Conflict Coach
notableEntities.whyNotable: Archie's first end-to-end build (~14,000 lines, 87% autonomous, hosted at conflictcoach.pages.dev); the proof case for the capacity-expansion pitch (Archie_Project.md).

notableEntities.name: Clarity
notableEntities.whyNotable: The v2 Archie system's from-scratch rebuild of Conflict Coach with 0% human-written production code (Archie_Project.md).

notableEntities.name: Lucien
notableEntities.whyNotable: His built memory system — verbatim conversation store feeding a synthesized wiki via nightly consolidation; the 'invisible memory' skill he iterates toward (AI_Memory_and_Agent_Systems.md; Lucien_Retrieval_and_Evaluation.md).

notableEntities.name: the Dreaming
notableEntities.whyNotable: The personal wiki Lucien produces (~/Dreaming/articles); refined Wikipedia-style with every claim cited back to the source conversation — the north-star artifact of his memory thesis (AI_Memory_and_Agent_Systems.md).

notableEntities.name: AlphaPoint
notableEntities.whyNotable: The crypto-exchange-infrastructure company where Josh works as a cross-functional generalist (~9 years, hired by the founder); the setting for the Archie pitch and his role/comp politics (AlphaPoint_Work_and_Politics.md).

notableEntities.name: Igor Telyatnikov
notableEntities.whyNotable: AlphaPoint's CEO; Josh's day-to-day operational-trust relationship (traveled alone with him to El Salvador to start Chivo) and a recurring named colleague (Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md colleague table; AlphaPoint_Work_and_Politics.md).

notableEntities.name: Chivo
notableEntities.whyNotable: The contested El Salvador national-wallet account renewal Josh built from zero with the CEO; a recurring high-stakes client engagement (AlphaPoint_Work_and_Politics.md).

notableEntities.name: Infrasight
notableEntities.whyNotable: A company in Josh's working orbit, tracked alongside AlphaPoint in his colleague reference (Myles Trachtenberg listed as Infrasight CTO) (Local_LLM_Inference_and_Deployment.md colleague table).

notableEntities.name: Toyota Production System
notableEntities.whyNotable: A recurring thinking lens (jidoka, kanban, andon) he grounds Archie's quality-and-compounding argument and the Mechanical Development Manifesto in (notability.json; Archie_Project.md).
